server.name: docker-docs
server.description: Search and retrieve Docker documentation to help with containerization, Docker Compose, Docker Hub, and related topics.
server.title: Docker Docs

tools.name: checkRepository
tools.description: Check if a repository exists in the given namespace.

tools.name: checkRepositoryTag
tools.description: Check if a tag exists in a repository

tools.name: createRepository
tools.description:
Create a new repository in the given namespace. You MUST ask the user for the repository name and if the repository has to be public or private. Can optionally pass a description.
IMPORTANT: Before calling this tool, you must ensure you have:
 The repository name (name).

arguments.name: namespace
arguments.desc: The namespace of the repository. Required.

arguments.name: description
arguments.desc: The description of the repository

arguments.name: full_description
arguments.desc: A detailed description of the repository

arguments.name: is_private
arguments.desc: Whether the repository is private

arguments.name: name
arguments.desc: The name of the repository (required). Must contain a combination of alphanumeric characters and may contain the special characters ., _, or -. Letters must be lowercase.

arguments.name: registry
arguments.desc: The registry to create the repository in

tools.name: dockerHardenedImages
tools.description: This API is used to list Docker Hardened Images (DHIs) available in the user organisations. The tool takes the organisation name as input and returns the list of DHI images available in the organisation. It depends on the "listNamespaces" tool to be called first to get the list of organisations the user has access to.

arguments.name: organisation
arguments.desc: The organisation for which the DHIs are listed for. If user does not explicitly ask for a specific organisation, the "listNamespaces" tool should be called first to get the list of organisations the user has access to.

tools.name: getRepositoryInfo
tools.description: Get the details of a repository in the given namespace.

arguments.name: namespace
arguments.desc: The namespace of the repository (required). If not provided the `library` namespace will be used for official images.

arguments.name: repository
arguments.desc: The repository name (required)

tools.name: getRepositoryTag
tools.description: Get the details of a tag in a repository. It can be use to show the latest tag details for example.

tools.name: listAllNamespacesMemberOf
tools.description: List all namespaces the user is a member of

annotations.title: List All Namespaces user is a member of

arguments.name: page
arguments.desc: The page number to list repositories from

arguments.name: page_size
arguments.desc: The page size to list repositories from

arguments.name: namespace
arguments.desc: The namespace to list repositories from

arguments.name: content_types
arguments.desc: Comma-delimited list of content types. Only repositories containing one or more artifacts with one of these content types will be returned. Default is empty to get all repositories.

arguments.name: media_types
arguments.desc: Comma-delimited list of media types. Only repositories containing one or more artifacts with one of these media types will be returned. Default is empty to get all repositories.

arguments.name: ordering
arguments.desc: The ordering of the repositories. Use "-" to reverse the ordering. For example, "last_updated" will order the repositories by last updated in descending order while "-last_updated" will order the repositories by last updated in ascending order.

arguments.name: page
arguments.desc: The page number to list repositories from

arguments.name: page_size
arguments.desc: The page size to list repositories from

arguments.name: repository
arguments.desc: The repository to list tags from

arguments.name: architecture
arguments.desc: The architecture to list tags from. If not provided, all architectures will be listed.

arguments.name: namespace
arguments.desc: The namespace of the repository. If not provided the 'library' namespace will be used for official images.

arguments.name: os
arguments.desc: The operating system to list tags from. If not provided, all operating systems will be listed.

arguments.name: page
arguments.desc: The page number to list tags from

arguments.name: page_size
arguments.desc: The page size to list tags from

tools.name: search
tools.description: Search for repositories in Docker Hub. It sorts results by best match if no sort criteria is provided. If user asks for secure, production-ready images the "dockerHardenedImages" tool should be called first to get the list of DHI images available in the user organisations (if any) and fallback to search tool if no DHI images are available or user is not authenticated.

arguments.name: query
arguments.desc: The query to search for

arguments.name: architectures
arguments.desc: The architectures to filter search results

arguments.name: badges
arguments.desc: The trusted content to search for

arguments.name: categories
arguments.desc: The categories names to filter search results

arguments.name: extension_reviewed
arguments.desc: Whether to filter search results to only include reviewed extensions

arguments.name: from
arguments.desc: The number of repositories to skip

arguments.name: images
arguments.desc: The images to filter search results

arguments.name: operating_systems
arguments.desc: The operating systems to filter search results

arguments.name: order
arguments.desc: The order to sort the search results by

arguments.name: size
arguments.desc: The number of repositories to return

arguments.name: sort
arguments.desc: The criteria to sort the search results by. If the `sort` field is not set, the best match is used by default. When search extensions, documents are sort alphabetically if none is provided. Do not use it unless user explicitly asks for it.

arguments.name: type
arguments.desc: The type of the repository to search for

tools.name: updateRepositoryInfo
tools.description: Update the details of a repository in the given namespace. Description, overview and status are the only fields that can be updated. While description and overview changes are fine, a status change is a dangerous operation so the user must explicitly ask for it.

arguments.name: namespace
arguments.desc: The namespace of the repository (required)

arguments.name: repository
arguments.desc: The repository name (required)

arguments.name: description
arguments.desc: The description of the repository. If user asks for updating the description of the repository, this is the field that should be updated.

arguments.name: full_description
arguments.desc: The full description (overview)of the repository. If user asks for updating the full description or the overview of the repository, this is the field that should be updated.

arguments.name: status
arguments.desc: The status of the repository. If user asks for updating the status of the repository, this is the field that should be updated. This is a dangerous operation and should be done with caution so user must be prompted to confirm the operation. Valid status are `active` (1) and `inactive` (0). Normally do not update the status if it is not strictly required by the user. It is not possible to change an `inactive` repository to `active` if it has no images.
